pending.body: In `matching_finalized_task_runs` (model.rs:448-480), the `started_at` fallback path does not exclude entries whose `run_id` is `Some(different_id)`. When a caller provides `run_id = Some("run-3")` and the DB has a finalized entry with `run_id = Some("run-2")` at the same `started_at`, the fallback returns that entry — blocking a legitimate new run with a misleading "already finalized" error. The fallback filter (line 476-479) should additionally require `entry.run_id.is_none()` when `run_id` was provided, so it only catches the runless-to-named upgrade case the tests intend to cover, not a collision between two distinct named runs that happen to share a timestamp.

`src/adapters/fs.rs:2295-2322` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2390-2469` merge completion journal events based only on `timestamp + event_type + bead_id` plus loosely compatible details. If two different attempts for the same bead/project complete at the same timestamp and at least one lacks a `run_id`, `append_event_if_missing` will repair/overwrite the first event instead of appending a second one. Tighten the dedupe key to a real attempt identity (for example `run_id`, or `started_at` when that is the explicit identifier) or drop this repair path.

run_snapshot.status_summary: running: final review restart round 20 -> Plan and Implement
